results.title: Bilt.me: AI Agent that builds real Native Mobile Apps | Product Hunt
results.content:
Get a real mobile app from your Figma design

You designed the whole thing in Figma. Then you had to hire someone to rebuild it by hand, pixel by pixel, and it came out "close enough." Not anymore. Drop your Figma frames into Bilt and they become a real app for iPhone. Your spacing, your fonts, your colors, exactly. You even own the code.

No-Code App Builder

Build and launch production-ready native mobile apps with AI. Prototype, collaborate, and deploy faster — no coding required. Build from scratch or auto deploy Web Apps into Mobile Apps. By making mobile app creation as quick and frictionless as writing a thought, we aim to unlock a wave of innovation where anyone can turn fresh ideas into working native apps the moment inspiration strikes.

This is the 3rd launch from Bilt.me. View more

## Bilt.me

Launching today

Get a real mobile app from your Figma design [...] Hey Product Hunt 👋 Every designer knows the heartbreak: you sweat every pixel in Figma, hand it off, and what ships back is… a vibe. The spacing drifted. The font's wrong. "We'll fix it later" (you won't). We got tired of it, so we built the Figma import for Bilt. Here's the whole thing: - Open your design in Figma, run the Bilt plugin, hit "Add this file to Bilt." Your frames show up in seconds, thumbnails and all. - Pick the exact screens you want. Anyone on the project can grab them. no Figma account required. - Bilt builds the real app. Not a screenshot, not a guess. Built from your actual design. You open it on your phone and send it straight to the App Store. A few things we're proud of: 1. What you design is what ships. Your spacing, fonts, and colors carry over exactly. No "close

# Figma Reviews

### The community submitted 1,504 reviews to tell us what they like about Figma, what Figma can do better, and more.

Based on 1,504 reviews

Review Figma? [...] Report

 129 views

•1 review

#### What's great

intuitive interface (52)prototyping capabilities (60)design systems (22)extensive plugin ecosystem (14)

Figma makes it incredibly easy to turn ideas into real product interfaces fast. The real-time collaboration is a game changer, especially when working with a team, and the prototyping tools let you test flows without friction. Everything feels intuitive, flexible, and built for iteration, it just gets out of the way and lets you build.

#### What needs improvement

With very large files, performance can sometimes slow down, and some advanced features have a bit of a learning curve at first. Offline usage is still limited, but overall these are small trade-offs compared to how powerful and efficient the tool is day to day. [...] Report

2. Export can be a pain with all the layers - always have to allocate some time to review and export again and again in case we missed an icon or a number or sth.
3. Offline mode is not the best - not ideal for folks who work on the go

results.title: App2.dev: Turn ideas & Figma designs into complete web & mobile apps | Product Hunt
results.content:
Hey Product Hunt! I'm Nick, the founder of App2 👋  
  
Today I'm excited to announce App2, the no-code platform that turns app ideas & Figma designs into complete web & mobile apps, is now open to the general public in early access!  
  
I built App2.dev because I believe everyone should be able to build marketable production-ready software, whether you're a non-technical founder with an idea, a designer who wants to prototype, or a developer who wants to move faster.  
  
What makes App2.dev different from Lovable, Cursor, Bolt, Replit, v0 and the likes, is not just the native Figma design parsing, predictive prompting or our UX, it's also our vision. [...] To answer your question: under the hood we're using GPT-5 Codex, Claude 4.5 Sonnet and Gemini 2.5 Pro. As we're moving forward, experienced users will be able to configure which models they want, as well as bring their own keys (which is currently only available on the Pro Lifetime plan).  
  
Potential local LLM support will also be implemented, depending on demand.

Exciting stuff ahead!

Vladimir Lugovsky
UI Bakery

UI Bakery

Congrats on the launch! Do you create native mobile apps or webview based?

Nick Gatzoulis
App2.dev

App2.dev

@vladimir\_lugovsky Thank you very much Vladimir! They're based on React Native. [...] App2.dev

## Figma Motion

Launched this week

Your Figma canvas now has a timeline [...] Report

12NextLast

4.9

Based on 1,503 reviews

Review Figma?

Reviewers largely see Figma as the default tool for UI work because real-time collaboration, easy sharing, browser access, and strong prototyping make design, feedback, and handoff much faster. Users also praise its intuitive feel, plugin ecosystem, components, auto-layout, and design-system support, though some say there is still a learning curve. The main complaints are familiar: lag on large or complex files, weak offline support, and some friction around exporting, version control, and pricing. Makers of Mintlify also highlight its value for detailed UX collaboration.

+1506

Summarized with AI

Pros

Cons

Reviews

All Reviews

Most Informative

Pros

real-time collaboration(170)

prototyping capabilities(60) [...] #### vs Alternatives

I evaluated Sketch, Adobe XD, and InVision before committing to Figma. What made Figma the clear winner: (1) Browser-based means zero friction for client collaboration, (2) Real-time multiplayer editing that actually works at scale, (3) The plugin ecosystem is unmatched, (4) Auto-layout and components are more powerful than competitors, (5) Cross-platform support means my team on Windows and Mac work seamlessly together. After 10 years in the industry and 200+ shipped products, Figma is the only tool I recommend without hesitation.

## UXPin

UXPin

The full-stack UX design platform

### Best for

Best for product teams that need code-based components and realistic, logic-driven prototypes to reduce handoff friction.

## Uizard

Uizard

App, web, & UI design made easy - powered by AI

Best for non-designers and early-stage teams that want AI-generated UI drafts for quick communication.
